channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 123 replies — 492.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 123 • Updated: 2026-05-29 05:46 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62282426
>>62280858
cryoto is better than everything

62282600
>>62276888
you would've sold them at $10

62282845
>>62271712
I just inherited almost a million usd and I'd like to put 100,000 into bitcoin but I don't know how :(

I'm also very concerned about getting ripped off :(

I also don't want to have to deal with taxes :(

Will you help me? Is there a way to buy it and somehow store it offline or is it only accessible online? Do I have to make some account at Binance or some other site?

62282847
>>62271712

62282849
>>62280044
There are many similar offerings ended up nothing so it's scary

62282854
>>62282845
Send it to me

62282864
>>62282845
kys
see
>>62282847

62282885
>>62282864
I don't wanna kill myself though. I just became a millionaire! Life is finally getting good for me :)

I just wanna buy a bitcoin. Why won't you help me?

62283041
>>62272050
Hmmm.. what do you mean?

62283050
>>62282849
That’s why people should wait for the actual TBV launch first instead of screaming revolution or scam this early.
At least the Aave v4 governance process is already moving now.

62283055
>>62272643
At least check it sometimes bro. You might already be retired and just don’t know it yet.

62283065
>>62272208
A 2x or 3x from here would still outperform most traditional investments by a huge margin.

62283375
>>62283050
Defi hacks are no joke

62283377
>>62282845
Why are you flexing your imagination?

62283380
>>62271712
Money

62283769
>>62276880
ARMA hasn't passed yet, but there's already a Reserve.

62283901
THE BIG DUMPING
>every cycle 90-70% loss. why would it be this time different.

62283926
>>62283901
diminishing returns mean diminishing drawbacks, retard

62283940
>>62282847
I hope that big brain stuck around, for my sake

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

62285517
>>62272225
They've got their preferences

62285578
>>62283375
Yeah, DeFi hacks are getting pretty crazy lately. That’s why multiple audits, formal verification, penetration testing, and cryptography reviews matter much more now with this much capital involved.

62285585
>>62272647
You'll regret your choices lol

62285589
>>62272667
You think you're smart?

62286335
>>62271712
No money, no job
They literally won't even take me at minimum wage places because I don't speak spanish.
So, no way to generate the income to buy BTC in the first place.

62287162
>>62274011
Work for it anon

62287177
>>62283952
You can already by McDonalds with corn. Use credit card and pay off credit card with corn futures. The problem is your dumbass is bleeding wealth by keeping your net worth in corn (or companies, or anything besides bitcoin) because people are going to make more corn (and ever faster each year due to tech advances) and inflate away your wealth.

62287383
>>62285578
but why remains the hack

62287392
>>62286335
If you're married at don't have money, you deserve a divorce

62287393
>>62271712
It's a gamble and I only bet on sure things. In fact I like to have my profits guaranteed under contract and insured BEFORE I spend a dime of my own money. I always sell BEFORE I buy so I know exactly how much I'm making in advance.

item.title: If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY? — 27 replies — 68.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY?

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-29 05:34 UTC

OP

If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY?

Early replies (first 3)

62286606
10k

62286609
8k

62286611
>>62286602
$10k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286614
>>62286602
$7500

62286617
>>62286602
2K

Maybe I think it would go private beiefly though so you may just get bought out for 2$ a share before it goes private again elon ah heroes and it's banrupted or bought by boeing for a dollar

62286623
And ignore emotional investing advice like >>62286617 btw

62286627
>>62286602
>▶▶
>>▶
It's not a scam, but I'd be very interested to see an IPO that didn't tank after the first few days.

62286630
>>62286623
UNAUDITED
>>>62286623
>emotional investing
YEAH?

62286634
>>62286630
Yeah

62286641
>>62286627
Coinbase hasn't even recovered to it's ipo kek

62286642
>>62286602
I bought Velo 3D, because they make spacex’s 3d printers. It’s been doing great.

62286651
>>62286602
6k

62286658
WELCOME TO THE GRAND COMMUNIST CIRCUS
Step right up, comrades! In today’s performance, everybody owns everything… which somehow means nobody can find toilet paper. HONK HONK.
First act: The government confiscates the bakery to “help the people.” Five minutes later the shelves looking like they got raided by raccoons. But don’t worry! A man in a giant red suit appears yelling, “Production has increased by 9000%!” while standing directly in front of an empty store. Audience applauds nervously.
Second act: The Amazing Disappearing Freedom Trick! One second you say, “Hey maybe this system ain’t working,” and POOF — now you taking an unscheduled vacation to a mysterious work camp somewhere cold enough to freeze soup solid. Incredible! Absolute cinema.
Third act: Equality for all! Everybody gets the same tiny apartment, the same sad bread loaf, and the same mandatory portrait of Supreme Mustache Leader smiling at you from every wall like a haunted mascot. Meanwhile the party elites eating steak backstage talking about “shared sacrifice.” HONK HONK HONK.
And the economy? Brother, the economy riding a unicycle on fire juggling potatoes. Every problem gets solved with another five-year plan written by twelve dudes who never touched a shovel in their life.
Communism really be like:“We fixed greed!”creates a bigger greedy government
Peak clown technology.

62286735
>>62286658
Dood elon musk is raising cows in space, spacecows!

62286776
>>62286602
$1,000,000

62286779
>>62286602
10k minus broker fees

62286790
>>62286735
Yo, SpaceX out here moving like that one dude in class who borrows money for lunch then flexes fake chains on Instagram. They screaming “future of humanity” while burning billions like it’s a mixtape launch party. Bro got rockets exploding on livestream so often people think it’s a Netflix trailer.

Meanwhile the finance students gotta calculate ROI, cash flow, and risk management while SpaceX be treating investor money like GTA cheat codes. “Yeah bro, we lost another rocket… but next one gonna be fire.” Literally.

Their whole business model sounds like a group project carried by government contracts. NASA basically the sugar daddy keeping the lights on while Elon tweets memes at 3 AM like he’s avoiding accounting homework. Imagine telling your professor: “My company valuation is vibes and Mars dreams.” You’d get escorted out the lecture hall instantly.

And don’t even start on the Mars talk. My guy can’t get reliable Wi-Fi on Earth but wants people paying rent on another planet. Finance professors teaching discounted cash flow while SpaceX valuation is discounted reality flow. Every launch got people acting like it’s the second coming of Einstein, then boom — another stainless steel bottle rocket doing cartwheels over Texas.

Street truth? SpaceX is that rich kid who crashes daddy’s Lambo, buys another one, and somehow gets called a genius entrepreneur. In any normal finance case study, half these decisions would be labeled “high-risk speculative behavior with catastrophic downside exposure.” But slap a cool rocket edit on TikTok and suddenly everybody forgets basic economics.

At this point the only thing reaching orbit consistently is the hype.

62286842
>>62286617
1.7 current ratio. I’m actually impressed. Not as bad as others made it out to be. I’ll invest a few grand.

62286883
>>62286790
Fuck off with your AI slop lol

62286906
>>62286790
You’re the reason we need data centers in space and will make spacex moon

62286916
Why does chatGPT output sound like a redditor with the "insufferable" settings at max?

62286955
>>62286916
He probably requested it

62286971
SPCE will 4x at least until the end of the year. It will be generational wealth in a decade. Your grandchildren will love you for set them for life.

62286991
>>62286602
1k

62287246
>>62286602
Between 1k and 20k

62287250
>>62286916
Where do you think they get all their AI training data from?

62287369
>>62286971
What about SPCX though

item.title: cardano, ADA is imploding right now — 51 replies — 49.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
cardano, ADA is imploding right now

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-05-29 04:56 UTC

OP

cardano, ADA
is imploding right now

Early replies (first 3)

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62275723
>>62275591
Charles-sama, why?

62276473
>>62275591
Imagine buying cardanus, ever! This shit has less than 500$ stables liquidity onchain.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278491
>>62278477
is Charles a jew? i always assumed he was a goy with all the western shit
>>62277725
i don't know, i sold all my ada in 2021.. but basically charles been scamming the treasury or something for years, makes sense, not surprised.. recently japanese validators or whatever voted to end funding the ""research"" laps funded by cardano.. charles apperently has always gone apeshit and burned every bridge but now it's become exponential bridge burnings and blockings..
ADA is a scam... nothing new.. but finally seeing Charles flame out in real time is kinda neat

62278891
>>62278491

62279915
The cope in this thread is unreal. Cry more, kiddies.

62280492
The narrative haven't really been evolving. Haven't seen your threads for some time and this is the buy signal I honestly was looking and not hoping for. Because the hate your try to project in inconsequential to you.

62280512
I'm not in ADA since 2021.
But now, i am daily scammed by BTC.
Dunno what's worse (financially) desu.

62280533
>>62277725
Some cheap twitter beef causing uproar. It's still one of the most technologically sound chains out there, but some people tried a power grab the minute Charles burnt his keys.
So the only mistake that Charles has made there is that he had too much good faith in those that are stabbing him in the back now. I guess that's what you get if you don't know who your real friends are.

62280595
jeets are still buying crypto lmao
lmfao even

62280622
>>62278448
This cope again
Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets” then maybe I’ll change my mind. Until then I think you’re delusional if you think it’s useful or “mass adoption” to have a handful of turbonerd billionaires and hedge funds use USDC or UST in place of traditional clearinghouses

62280637
>>62277444
But muh Haskell

62280714
>>62280622
>Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets”
DTCC is going to use Chainlink for stablecoins too you know.

62280809
>>62278477
It's a pretty good heuristic

62280833
Save what value you can, swap your ADA for XMR and hodl!

62281020
>>62280595
crypto is banned in india
so in a way, you're like a jeet

62281159
I never trusted Charles so I never bought cardano…dude is an obvious psycho

62281608
ENJOY YOUR CARDANO, EARTHMAN!

62281639
>>62275591
>@TheCardanoTimes

62281653
>>62281159
This, one look at that basedboi faggot should tell you all you need to know

If you still made the decision to put money in this project, its your fault, really

62282581
>>62277151
fucking kek charles is such a fucking narcissist

62282732
>>62277151
Wow that's very bold and stupid. Homeboy thought he could just jump into an industry as complex as healthcare. "Mayo Clinic of the West" lmao

62282767
>>62278448
token not needed

62282801
>>62280714
No they aren't.

62282929
>>62282801

62284091
>>62275698
>>62277437
buy signal

62284100
>>62281020
>crypto is banned in india
just googled it and you're full of shit

62284157
>>62277590
>I remember you could play Super Mario 64 on the Internet Computer Protocol (bing bing wahoo on ICP, jej) but who the fuck cares?
The whole point of that experiment killed ICP the moment it was taken off. What's the point of your web infrastructure if regular web rules still apply to it? Why the fuck is your product worth dick if you're just reinventing the wheel?
>buh muh token costs!!
Irrelevant. If price to run the grid increases because token become more valuable, then whole economic incentive to migrate to your web infrastructure falls apart.

62284265
>>62275591
did they ever get dapps?

62284324
>>62284091
>buy signal

under $20k btc

62284332
>>62284265
i don't think so, who wants to learn haskell

62287225
lol, lmao, etc

62287288
>still worth more than xlm
Tragic

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 20 replies — 42.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-29 05:30 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285966
>>62285713
TP? Where are you? And do you still own BAT?

62286043
>>62285619
bat og here. im heavily underwater with my bat investment at this point but still holding. i'm never going to sell - not because bat will be some kind of currency in the future and i won't need to sell it like some link cope, but I don't want to make profit from BAT anymore and I also don't want anyone to use my tokens for ill purposes. truth be told, after reading that brendan eich in 2006 didn't think that people with xy chromosomes that undergo hrt and srs maybe shouldn't be allowed to compete in female chess tournaments has really had me rethinking my investment and the morality of it. i only want to invest in ethical companies like lockheed martin that actually help make democracy more accessible to global citizens around the world. if over 20 years ago the ceo of brave browser had such a controversial view it makes me concerned that he might have some problematic ideas today. did he even get the covid vaccine? im not sure, and I don't want to find out the hard way. i will simply hold onto my hundreds of thousands of bat, and not sell them, not lend them, just freeze them up on my hard drive in a password i refuse to commit to memory and dispose of my thinkpad into landfill. i encourage all other bat holders with a conscience to do the same.

62286053
>>62286043
Wow. That's some deep pasta bro. Missed this one

62286063
>>62285713
>IT'S BEEN A WHILE
It's been a hot minute. Sup, lad?

>>62285966
>Where are you?
o/
>do you still own BAT?
Of course, lad *sobs *

Haven't seen a thread in a minute. Was planning on doing some thot bait threads when we get a new JanJan.

62286087
>>62286063
>Blue hair
Gross

62286122
>>62286087
I mean if you want me to post men or trannies for you, just give me the word ^_^
_

Anyone else getting some pretty gnarly memory leaks from Brave recently?

62286148
>>62286122

62286153
>>62286063
>>62286122
Hawt
Got any uber thicc goths??

62286226
BAT is total shite. You have to be retarded to still believe in this garbage

62286297
>>62285619
if i had invested in LINK instead of BAT i'd be a millionaire right now, and probably retired

62286350
>>62286122
no fucking trannies please.

just hot ass bitches, keep em coming TP, and thanks legend

62286359
>>62286297
If I ate one pile of dogshit instead of another pile of dogshit I'd be puking my brains out harder right now too

62286450
>>62286148

>>62286153
Of course!

>>62286297
I absolutely abhor LINK, but same u_u. I literally should've put more money anywhere else.

>>62286350
I'm just fucking around.
>Legend
Much appreciated!

>>62286359
At least STINK pumped. All I wanted was $4 BAT.

62286624
>>62286450
fuark that bitch can get it

62286763
>>62286450
Would happily suffocate devouring her ass while she sits on my face 10/10 slut

62287337

62287362

item.title: UH OH WAGIES — 21 replies — 38.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
UH OH WAGIES

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-29 05:25 UTC

OP

Bezos' rocket just blew up

Early replies (first 3)

62286977
>>62286974
well at least it looked cool

62286979
>>62286974
>>62286977
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62286993
buying the amazon dip tomorrow

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287004
We have to keep relearning the Challenger disaster I guess.

62287005
elon was in the replies gloating, what a cunt

62287007
i guess more layoffs coming then

62287014
>>62287005
>NOOOOOO DON'T MAKE FUN OF MY HECKIN ROCKET! NOOOOOOOOOO

62287024
Manay ominous signs this past week

62287030
>>62286974
$100 million vaporized in a few seconds.

62287042
>>62286974
Musk must be laughing his ass off

62287046
>>62287007
Either this or emergency unpaid overtime shifts.

62287049
KWAB

62287052
CLEAN UP THE DEBRIS, WAGIE!!!

62287125
>>62286974
bezos can suck the shit of my asshole

62287128
>>62286974
So? Eill stonks go down and crypto up now? No? So?

62287260
>>62286974
AMAZON x XRP PARTNERSHIP?????????

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62287271
>>62287052
what an ugly freak god damn nigga even with 270 billion dollars being this ugly has got to be rough.

62287278
Gender studies be one of them classes where you realize society really out here making random rules then acting like them rules dropped from the sky . The whole subject talks about how people get treated different based on gender, race, class, and identity. It ain’t just about “men versus women.” Nah, it’s deeper than that. It’s about power, who got it, and who keeps the system running like a broken carnival ride .

Back in the day, society pushed these roles heavy. Dudes supposed to be tough, work jobs, never cry. Girls supposed to stay pretty, quiet, cook food, and smile through suffering like customer service employees on minimum wage . Gender studies basically walks into the room and says, “Yo, who invented these goofy rules?” That’s where people start talking about gender as a social construct. Meaning society made a lot of these expectations up over time and everybody just downloaded the update without reading the terms and conditions .

Another big thing in gender studies is intersectionality. Big word, simple meaning. Different struggles stack together like bad Wi-Fi connections. Somebody can face problems because of gender, race, money, or all three at once. The system don’t hit everybody equally. Some people start life on easy mode while others spawn into expert difficulty with no tutorial .

Media also catches strays in this class because movies, ads, music, and social media keep pushing stereotypes 24/7. Folks grow up seeing the same roles repeated until they think it’s “normal.” Gender studies tries to challenge those dominant narratives and make people question why society moves the way it does.

At the end of the day, the class teaches that identity, power, and culture all connected. Also teaches students how capitalism be profiting off stereotypes while everybody else just trying to survive and pay rent .

62287284
NO BATHROOM BREAKS TOMORROW

12 HOUR SHIFTS SAT AND SUN

62287347
>>62287260
so bullihs at this..

62287354
>>62287271
He literally looks like any other MF in the street

item.title: ICP has superior technology — 14 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
ICP has superior technology

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-29 05:02 UTC

OP

All crypto today is fucking snakeoil except ICP. That’s why they manipulated the launch price with futures and tried to kill Dom’s project. Amazon and google are afraid of real competition.

Early replies (first 3)

62285029
>>62285022
Solana is a scam.

The only legit alts out there are ICP and their partner HBAR. You can make an argument for XRP Hyperliquid and link

62285041
>>62285022
imagine if this shit somehow end up mooning and i didn't buy any despite your endless shilling while it was 2$

62285042
>6 month old fogo has 3x the transactions of 11 year old eth
true and real

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285065
Betamax has superior picture quality than VHS. Betamax will be the future.

62285158
>>62285041
Yeah it's going to explode, it's going to go to $0.60 and then back up to $2.20 and the board will be full of euphoric ICP bots.

62285239
Utility and token price are not directly connected and when there is a vast amount of premined tokens in reserve just waiting to be dumped its in the companies best interest to suppress the price so that there is higher utilization.

62285307
>INTERNET COMPUTER


Cycles / s
33,667,085,111
+

Network market cap
$1,565,741,556
+
§Internet Computer Protocol (ICP)
Sovereign internet cloud for AI that builds
Tamperproof · Always-on · Yours

The Internet Computer is a frontier cloud created by a mathematically secure network protocol that combines independently operated compute nodes. Hosted apps and services are immune to infrastructure hacks, and guaranteed to run, so AI agents can build and update them hands-free, without the need for human security and systems teams in the loop. Frontier serverless languages introduce "orthogonal persistence," which enables agents to build sophisticated backends faster, using fewer compute tokens, while preventing lossy app updates that might lose data. Digital assets ready.
This is absolutely incoherent Babble

62285845
>>62285022
i possess ICP and I am a homosexual

62285861
I still believe in Moonman I don’t care what anybody says, he was racist and therefore based so I believe in him fully a racist would never EVER act like a nigger and lie

62286056
>>62285022
The real question is how did crypto somehow miss all of the gains from AI hype. Some coins pumped like 20%-30% off the narra and that was it. Can't help but hold on to hopium that more will come for AI related coins, but I know better teebs.

62287044
>>62285022
better table would be value in satoshis transferred.

62287272
>>62285042
In no universe is this real, what the fuck is this thread?

62287289
>>62286056
Crypto is almost entirely retail, while the stock market has a much higher institutional presence. I guess the current AI push is driven by institutions. What retail does on an entirely different market is unrelated.

62287299
>>62285065
>the great Betamax rug pull of 1975

item.title: ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly — 11 replies — 9.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-29 04:45 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62285243
>>62285128
crazy the amount of time people spend fudding a random alt coin.
it happens so much, the amount of energy that's spent on it buy a group of individuals, or a bot? either way, it's very interesting from the outside
made me start to think the linkies are onto something..
to anyone else on the outside, like anyone here from pmg or smg? how does this look? pretty crazy to me..
anyway the constant fud against link made me think i should secure a suicide bag of it, and now i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
so must be trying to hide something

62285245
quick, make more threads!

62285257
>>62285128
so true, king

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285535
>>62285243
>i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
STFU nigga you aint doin that dont lie fees would rape u

62285592
>>62285535
like 1-2 cent a buy?

62285749
>>62285128
>chainlink staking mechanism colorized

62285799
>>62285128
Ikyk

62285888
>>62285243
Link monkeys are Lolcows. DUH.

62287261
>>62285888
anymore then bbby or gamestop guys? yet you don't see 20 threads fudding or shitting on them everyday?
it's pretty weird..
i have less then 500 link btw, at average of 7-8, i literally don't care if it moons or not, just one of many "if the schizos are right' small bags
plus we all learned link triggers you transgenic, homos so much, hence my continued dca, despite knowing nothing about chainlink or dtcc or orcale (thought that was a different company)

item.title: ETH price prediction. $40000 by 2030 — 6 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
ETH price prediction. $40000 by 2030

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-29 05:14 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62287147
>>62287139
Rookie numbaz, make .2 ethbtc and its believable.

62287188
>>62287139
4,000 in 2026 lol

62287222
I'm barely holding on to $2 million net worth now. If ETH gets to $10k and BTC to $200k, I'll definitely be set for life.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287240
Gold going to 7000 when?

62287255
>>62287222
You're already set for life, nigger.

62287328
>>62287139
The Indian gentleman who made this chart never heard of the 4-year cycle

>>62287222

item.content:encoded:
lever up on XLM now.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-29 05:00 UTC

OP

this pile of shit is always good for the yearly crime candle and it's due with the DTCC news.
>Only coin in the top 100 green today
>Massive spike in volume on spot and perps (2nd day)
They'll crime this one for a at least a few more days. Time to make your money back anon. Ogs remember the 2021 xlm crime candle that kickstarted the bull cycle.

Early replies (first 3)

62283130
buncha noobs also going to short it cus muh mean reversion everything else is crashing!

62283353
>>62283344

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283356
>>62283117

62283608
>>62283356

62283617

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 53 replies — 47.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-05-29 04:52 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285426
>>62285389
>1% sl, 80% volume reduction and 10x leverage i would have made 1B
I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
>what is trading volatility? derisking by how much?
up to your own style. Like I said, the demo strategy just sells 50% on Volatility confirmation, since the goal of that strategy is just to show how an almost-passive investor can make gains with 4-6 trades a month
One user scalps ETH liquidation levels using it as a bull/bear bias and says he's printing
>if you make me money, i'll gladly give you 5-10% of my profits
no need anon, I'm not charging anyone money until I've got the model tuned into ridiculous accuracy. maybe give the twitter a follow and turn notes on, leave a like or comment on the posts, that'd be a big help

62285441
>>62285363
Ah I see. But if you sell at red and buy at green you shouldn't be holding any at yellow anyways. So derisk should only really happen if you're shorting.

Anyways my experience with short term trading crypto is you get eaten alive by the fees. Not only are the fees exorbitantly higher than for stocks but you get charged per dollar not per coin/share. For example on kraken you have to be doing over a million in monthly volume just to get your fees down to a reasonable rate and even thats assuming you never make market orders.

62285468
You guys still use meme indicators? I simply look at the moon

62285477
>>62285441
>But if you sell at red and buy at green you shouldn't be holding any at yellow anyways. So derisk should only really happen if you're shorting.
The Volatility signals are weird inflection points where you can get either early trend reversals, or sudden trend accelerations - combining it with the FX layer is really good at identifying which of those it's likely to be, and confirming/invalidating the signal.
For example,
>>62285172
Here you got a Buy followed by a Volatility with FX counter-trend to price trend (negative) - the implication is exactly what happened, a short-term bullish impulse
>>62284814
The May 25 Sell was followed by the 26th Volatility with inline FX to price trend (negative) - implies acceleration to the downside, and man did that happen

62285480
>>62285468
Why look at the moon when you can look at the wave anon

62285577
how do i find this? website?

62285614
>>62285577
Watermark

62285615
>>62285426
>I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
yeah, most likely. Definitely tune it, it's super useful to understand how tight should the strategy be.

explain me something, i'm seeing two future signals (may 29 and may 30), this won't change if i understand correctly, that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website? but the confirmation layer will only be updated in that day, yes? I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
Finally when are the trades done? Immediately when a new daily candle starts? Or is there any rule?

i'm going to give you a follow for sure

62285636
>>62285615
>that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website?
First one that confirms. If FX continues this positive tomorrow, 2h after US open it'll confirm. Honestly I think buys today are good but that's not what the forecast back on the 3rd said
> I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
The FX gauges are the confirmation layer, Strategy is an oscillator using several weighted factors (physics predictions, FX on different timeframes, seasonality, etc) that's supposed to give you a simple, accurate bias for the day. It's all WIP but in its defense it was screaming Strong Sell on the 25th and man was that accurate
>Finally when are the trades done?
They execute immediately on confirmation, right now I'm working on a fully automated bot that just uses Strategy and a basic Bollinger strat for entries/exits
>i'm going to give you a follow for sure
thanks anon! Likes and comments on the posts are maybe the biggest possible help right now

62285706
>>62285636
is there anything i can read about the schizo psychics this is based on?

62285733
>>62285706
From the other thread, Econophysics as a concept has been around since Bacherie's "Theorie de la speculation" in 1900, but what I'm doing is based on "Price variations in a stock market with many agents" from 1997 as a foundation, and "Financial brownian particle in the layered order-book fluid and fluctuation-dissipation relations" from 2014, and then applying a pretty simple schizo concept to take the reasoning one step further. The end result is predictive weeks in advance

62285770
>>62285733
Gonna look into it
tks anon

62285791
>>62285480
The moon creates the tides

62285803
>>62285791
>wild shrimp need tides to reproduce
>moon creates tides
>moon makes shrimp sandwiches possible
Thanks moon

62285830
>>62285103
>https://shiyu-coder.github.io/Kronos-demo/
this shit looks overfit nigga id backtest it at least

62285839
I have a similar model coded up in pine it is quite useless lol

62285878
>>62285839
lmao this one works a little better, my main problem was underestimating the violence we got on fairly faint signals this last 3 days, which might be partially the nature of the simulation - outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Still, calling every major pivot this month using schizo math is pretty decent

62285897
>>62285878
shut up bot

62285919
>>62285897
no u

62285940
>>62285919
you probably work for the cia retard

62285967
>>62285940
>CIA
>giving away the free money cheat code to internet autists
doesn't seem very likely anon

62286025
>>62285967
I mean as long as this doesn't go full premium (as a ploy to ensnare gullible /biz/ tards) it seems like a good resource. Some ppl on this board want to stay poor or just FUD for fun. Pathetic behavior honestly.

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62286532
>>62284814
>buy may 17
>sell for a loss on May 25

thanks

62286552
>>62286532
>buy may 17
>short may 25
you're welcome

62286639
>>62286447
>because it's a lot easier
Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
>I'm a nerd, not a trader
So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.

I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.

62286700
>>62286639
>Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
A lot of it is! I keep getting tempted to outsource some of it to an LLM but some gut instinct says "you'd be really really dumb to do that," even a local one
>So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.
Oh, I use it, have been trading perps with it for years, getting more profitable as the model evolves. After the addition of the FX layer last spring, I can get away with some truly disgusting leverage. I just meant I don't have a background in trading or technical analysis, so I've been learning all of those skills on the go. It's all related but still a different skillset
>I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.
Please do, I'll post in here when there's updates but you can always get in touch via Twitter

62287275
>>62284814
I’m just gonna wait for the mega dump to flush the longs down to 60k..

62286128

Trump also made a Truth post yesterday explicitly mentioning

62286180
ah there it is just confirms it.

62286206

62286304

62286343

62286514
>>62285810

62286671
The nano second the Glamsterdam update releases there will be 3 hyperliquid competitors launching, with instant access to $40 Billion in liquidity on L1 (Hype only have $2 billion). JEFF WE RUE THE DAY HE BETRAYED VITALIK BY LAUNCHING A SPERATE EVM L1.

62286723

62286733

62286743
>>62286723

62286801

62286820
>>62286801
so what, it isnt random vc niggers getting the unlocks, it's the team and they've shown they dont turbo dump like sirgay and his band of faggots

62286960
>>62286801
Also constant burns

62286967
>>62286820
This is what makes hyperliquid different. It was just created by a turbo autist that loves perp futures trading, he gave everything to the community, no VCs that dump everytime it pumps, no HR team, no marketing, nothing, just a product that works and people actually use it.

It's the most used blockchain, it generates the highest amount of fees and the buyback mechanism makes it pretty hard to crash. Now, with stocks and commodities, everytime there is a happening, you have everyone trading the current thing, gold, silver, oil, IPO's, whatever, and it brings even more people to hyperliquid. It's ATHs so everybody made money with hype.

One of the safest bets right now in crypto.

62286984
I waiting for the big drop in November so I will fill my pockets with SOLANA and hold it until 2029.

62286986
>>62286967

62287228
>>62286986

62287229
>>62287228
ouch

item.title: >turning 31 >just got a CDL I'm a loser and don't have many other options, but part of me is having cold feet because I… — 56 replies — 24.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>turning 31 >just got a CDL I'm a loser and don't have many other options, but part of me is having cold feet because I…

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-05-29 03:40 UTC

OP

>turning 31
>just got a CDL
I'm a loser and don't have many other options, but part of me is having cold feet because I know that once I do this, the door to doing anything else basically closes because if I change my mind in five years I'll be nearly 40 and trying to start over again. I just want a remote job doing anything even if it's lower pay but not completely dead end; tried dabbling in coding and accounting but the threat of AI taking away the low value human capital positions made me lose a lot of motivation

Early replies (first 3)

62286399
>>62286379
>accountant
You need a very specific type of intelligence and worth ethic to be an accountant. lol.
You, a failure at 40 and trucking as an option, accounting definitely isnt for you.

62286405
>>62286379
I grew up in a very good area with one of the best school districts in the country in the first world.
I can 100% say I have a very good perspective on many things.
You need to be a certain type of person to be in accounting.
Hard to explain but if you know you know.
I grew up in a good area and from grade 1 to 12, I already knew the types that got straight A’s in class and exams and etc.

62286410
>>62286405
You don't need good grades rip be an accountant

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286645
You earned a CDL.

You can work towards something else.

Just don't stop and never be a lazy piece of shit.

You;ll be good.You realize you typed this shit on $skins right. Good luck yo!

62286668
ITT
Anons who don’t want to admit characteristics and traits dictate what profession you go into.
Just like how blue collar wagies don’t want to admit they are barely 70 iq monkeys.
Go walk into a construction site in your shithole town, every worker there looks like a golem.

62286690
>>62286668
ITT anon tries to twist accounting from the paperwork equivalent of Sisyphus where gay ass guidelines are mounted more and more and one little fuckup blows up the whole mountain to "no but seriously these redditors are the best and the brightest, you have to get all of the best medals at star academy".
I switched to statistics for a reason, accounting is the janitor of banking.
>>62286379
Don't go into accounting, you'll shoot up the place within a month (the school or whatever poor takes you as an intern), also they're replacing the entry level shit with pajeets, especially the big 4.

62286691
>>62286644
Yea muh dick. Lot lizards will blow him while you’ve never had a girl touch your dick

62286710
>>62286691
Kinda hoping he is better than that. But if that's his this thing, why not?

Don;t do that Anon.

62286748
>>62286379
i do feel sorry for you because trucking can be demanding and driving is dangerous, but at the same time i've heard of truckers living great lives so i don't feel bad for you. you should have gotten a bachelors in something if you didnt want a working job.

62286759
>>62286748
So this example is from the boomer period but I remember one of my dad's friends going into trucking instead of using his engineering degree because he simply could not take the stress of living in a major metro. Like his life wasn't all sunshine and rainbows but he made do. Get a truck fridge full of reta and chicken breast, you won't get too fat.

62286761
Truckers deliver all the stuff we want to buy.

Hang in there. Drive safe yo!

Stay alert and take it seriously.

62286783
>>62286477

This is the most autistic board.

62286785
Lol this thread is a good reflection of society.
No one wants to admit they are low status even though their job is exactly that. low status.

62286786
>>62286690
>accounting is the janitor of banking
kek

62286826
I agree with the personality guy. Most jobs require other personality traits than just being smart to do well. Really want to find something that is a good fit for your brain and environment if possible or you are going to have a bad time.

62286828
>>62286785
OP started his post by saying he is a loser. That set up this thread to be that way. In the future stop posting like a loser and fag. No offense OP. People need to stop being afraid to post proud. If you want to be a trucker, just say I'm a trucker! Then maybe talk shit about people who aren't truckers. Let's have fun and not be pussys.

Start acting like men! Tell some bullshit story about fucking people's mom"s who are lot lizards. Just kiding, be respectful. Stand up and take pride in your work.

62286834
>>62286618
I can stop being an accountant whenever this dogshit actually pumps
>>62286605
Yea getting that first experience is the hardest part but it sets you up well to get a good job afterwards

62286845
>>62286379
Trucking is tough job, try your best to at least get good nutrition. I suggest meal prep and have a freezer and mini fridge in your cab that way you are not always eating out, also make sure you do burpees and some push-ups and pull-ups. You need to get some exercise and movement in to pump spinal fluid throughout your back or you will get back problems. You don’t have to do a lot, just like 20-30 minutes of some good exercise a day. It’ll be awkward as you will have to do in parking lot but you must do what you gotta do. Do these things and you will survive trucking without turning into a fat piece of shit

62286849
>>62286828
That dude would have called me a loser anyway, he just lurks around here looking for threads to spam in

62286853
>>62286845
Yeah that's my biggest concern. I don't think thirty minutes a day would compensate for 10 hours of pure sitting. At least I'm an office job you're not completely chained to the desk

62286864
>>62286379
>the door to doing anything else basically closes because if I change my mind in five years I'll be nearly 40 and trying to start over again
So what? You're going to be dead in about 50 years anyways. If you hate driving and want to change careers at 40, just do it. Life is too short to stick with a job you hate because you feel like you sunk too much time and money into it to switch tracks.

62286865
>>62286826
People don’t want to admit they lack certain traits for certain jobs.
>>62286849
You’re fucking stupid. Pearls before swine.
I gave you tons of good feedback but your blue collar ass couldn’t handle it. You missed the forest for the trees.
A studious guy would NOT fit into blue collar work.
A blue collar guy wouldn’t fit into an office environment downtown commercial building.
Cultural differences. To be put it this way so low iq mid wits don’t get offended.

62286874
>>62286865
I didn't ignore any of your advice retard. It doesn't apply to me, I'm aware that Big 5 personality traits are more important than IQ and I never claimed that I wanted to become an accountant. But despite posting 20 times you ignored me when I called out your incredibly poor reading comprehension

62286889
>>62286874
>muh big 5 personality traits
Lmao.
Omg.
You’re such a low iq retard. Lmao.

62286892
>>62286865
>studious guy would NOT fit into blue collar work
Then why was my CDL school full of dudes trying to leave those positions to become truckers? And an entry level accounting position at a small firm or doing something like account receivable is not rocket science, I took classes on it and it's pretty straightforward but it is tedious

I think you have a mental condition, jeet

62286895
>>62286874
An accountant is very different from a truck driver.
If you don’t want to admit this, you are either being defensive or the typical blue collar type who pretends he is just the same if not better than everyone else.

62286902
>>62286892
>entry level
Lmao. Just like how colleges hand out student loans and even those that should not be in college go to college.
You’re so fucking stupid.
Those people that switched were blue collar by blood and had no business in white collar. Fuck. You’re either a mid wit dealing in the real world or you’re pretending “everyone is equal and can be anything they want.”

62286904
>>62286889
You come off as low IQ just through text. Idgaf about your laro or your advice. Go stink up another thread

62286909
>>62286902
>blue collar by blood
100% Jeet coded

62286927
>>62286904
>You come off as low IQ just through text. Idgaf about your laro or your advice. Go stink up another thread
Says the retard thinking of working in trucking. lmao. The jokes write themselves.
>>62286892
> took classes on it and it's pretty straightforward but it is tedious
Wait till you start studying for certifications and not some basic program. Seperates the cum from the stain. You’re just the stain.

62286934
>>62286892
> And an entry level accounting position at a small firm or doing something like account receivable is not rocket science, I took classes on it and it's pretty straightforward but it is tedious
Getting to the next level like true accountants is where the fun starts. You’re just a retard who should have went straight into blue collar work instead of larping as white collar.
You don’t even know your own weaknesses and you think you’re intelligent or the studious type.
It takes a certain type plus determination to study for those exams to get the official certifications.
Any retard can finish a basic 4 year college program.
Studious or “smart” or nerdy people graduate with high gpa.
Blue collar people even if not dumb, don’t have what it takes to study for hours and hours at a table and desk.

62286950
Let’s argue amongst ourselves. Are we working? Just do what you are capable and are happy doing.

No need to shit talk. Let’s use this board to do business. If someone says they would like to pursue a career in a certain field, the people in that field should reach out and help them. That would be productive. Be useful and support those who need help.


Let’s be a board that is successful!

62287127
>>62286934
>me reading the log of pride incarnate
Mop up the books, janny.
>>62286909
Look, I was in a similar position where I graduated and wanted to try a bunch of things. I don't need to tell you what to do because you know deep inside. Do you want to truck and deal with the long term isolation and lesser health outcomes after decades of hard work or would you rather suck it up socially, study and put in the work to be somewhat competent and start building your credentials from there? You know the answer. I can't dictate your station in life, and especially Mr. Gay Pee In G-Spot (KPMG, I tried okay?). But if you do get an accounting degree, if you want the good bucks, you're gonna have to get the certifications. Accounting is a high stress environment; people are miserable, despite their internal narratives might say otherwise. Do an IQ test, if you lean spatial, and your verbal is below 110 (a lot of accounting is reading guidelines), consider something else. I believe in you, OP. :D

item.title: IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever! — 50 replies — 20.8/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-05-29 03:34 UTC

OP

IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Early replies (first 3)

62283021
>>62283018
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283022
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283030
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283598
>>62283039

62283626
>>62283598
Dividing SPX by the USM2 is almost the same thing as adjusting to inflation.

62283790
>>62283411
How do you know which stock market might be the next Nikkei? It went up, until it didn't, and then took 35 years to recover

62283830
>>62283790

All of them

62284284
>>62283018

62284502

62284646

62284879
>>62284502

62284961
>>62284646

do goyim really?

62285025
>>62283021
>>62284284

whats the alternative?
>inb4 just buy gold

62286115
>>62284502
Kek

>>62285025
The market can keep on being exuberant for many months, so keep more and more cash by your side, a play that’s too contrarian like leveraged shorts could wipe you

62286525
>>62283018
>IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Until it doesn't.

62286553
>>62283258
>>Your current holdings get rekt by 90%
That never happened though. And you can just hodl and it usually bounces back within. A few months like OP's chart shows.

62286561
Wagmii

62286596
>>62283018
>IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Except for where it goes down

62286638
>>62286525
Literally the best time to buy though. How do you think gains are made.

62286688
>>62286638
Boughted at peak - fucked for 25 years

62286712
>>62286688
Wow you only ever got income once in 25 years and it corresponded to a market peak? What a wild life you've lived.

62286756
>Wow you only ever sold your house once in 25 years?
>Wow you only ever inherited once in 25 years?

62286769
>put brexit on the graph like england actually matters
holy delusional little broski wilted flower

62286806
>>62286688
Investing in 1935 looks comfy. 32 and 33 you had to perfectly time it to catch the bottom.

62286847
>>62286688
The Nikkei 225 is ever better, 35 years before recovering if you bought the top in 1989

62286861
Listen folks, diversify, you want at least 3 different stocks

62286914
>>62286596
If assumed to be linear we still have a ways to go before we reach the peak

62286919
>>62286756
You've never heard of DCA and you're being smug about it

62286996
>>62286919
But Vanguard research taught us that:

>Lump-sum investment strategies beat common cost averaging investment
strategies two-thirds of the time, according to historical and simulated
market data

This is science, you must go all in with all your funds immediately, just unplug your brain and buy the top!!!

62287000
>>62286996
Sorry for botched greentext lol

62287020
>>62285025

62287086
>>62283018
check the Nikkei 225

62287105
>>62283018
>just pick the right ones at all times

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 137 replies — 16.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 137 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:38 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283730
>>62283715
>Either Hedera "servers" interact with them ("step 1")
No the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera.
> or they integrate Hedera's code into their base chain code ("step 2").
It's not "Hedera's code" whatever the fuck that means.

Two ledgers can talk with each others without having to interact with Hedera at all. Cope all you want it's just how it is.

62283741
>>62283730
>the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera
They're Hedera's servers. The servers run Hedera's code.

>It's not "Hedera's code"
CLPR is coded by Hedera. It's Hedera's code.

62283782
>>62283741
Two ledgers can talk with each other via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.

Keep coping jew.

62283798
>>62283782
>via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.
Not talking about the DLT, I'm talking about the project.

62283808
>>62283798
So again, why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level? This makes CCIP and all other bridges obsolete.

62283817
>>62283702
>>62283730
>>62283782
Why do you keep larping about the technicals when they havent been released?

62283819
>>62283808
Firstly because no other chain wants to alter their base code and functionality by injecting someone else's shit.
Secondly because it's retarded to ask blockchain validators/miners/nodes/... to moonlight as oracles.

62283824
>>62283817
Did you even read
https://hashgraph.com/blog/clpr-a-new-bridgeless-standard-for-cross-ledger-communication/
?
I know it's not in depth but it's something

62283873
>>62283824
yeah I screwed up, i meant the guy you were talking to and one other retard. the only rebuttal so far has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible. i think this clpr thing is not getting the serious consideration from CLL it deserves.

62283889
>>62278023
lmao i didnt report shit lol
>>62278047
swapping ids? where faggot? i posted the op in my car, now im on wifi. made that very clear early on.

i get it you guys married your bags, but I didnt and until this is addressed by CLL im going to continue to assume there’s a serious contender until it is addressed by serg.

62283896
>>62283678
you people are so weird constantly id hopping, it gives away that you're jeets, nobody else would care that much on this dead board.
also to the other two faggots, anon engaged you seriously and you gave the game away by getting mad at him, you're not genuine link holders you're hbar baggies spamming the board. you faggots did this once before when link first joined that faggy council trying to act like friends and getting people to buy your bags. hbar is never going to be a thing here, leemon is never going to be a meme, go somewhere else (although i get you're paid to be here so it is what it is)

62283902
>>62283873
What I'm talking about is literally from the documentation.
In all cases, this system requires the introduction of external data from one chain to another.

Step 1 involves "dedicated servers", which basically means third party oracles.

Step 2 involves ledgers implementing CLPR "natively" whereby the validators become the endpoints.
Meaning the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... have to operate as oracles.

62283908
>>62283873
>has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible.
lmao that is exactly what you faggots are doing in this and like 20 other threads on the board right now. meanwhile anon actually rebuts everything and you faggots just pilpul and pretend you didn't hear previous responses.

62283922
>>62283896
>>62283908
>there can be NO questions
keep coping faggot. count my posts and suck these nuts.
>>62283902
ok then post the fucking sauce

62283946
>>62283922
>ok then post the fucking sauce
The sauce is all over this thread

62283951
>>62283922
you are an hbar baggie, you are a faggot, hbar will never ever be a thing

62284227
>>62283896
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62284232
>>62284227
>1pbtid
keep swapping

62284314
>>62283922
It seems like nobody here really knows anything about CLPR. Hell they didn't even know it existed two days ago lol.

62284575
>>62278842
Wow another private key hacked. Chainlink would have prevented this.

62284723
>>62284232
lick my ass, nigger

62284748
>>62284723
>lick my ass, nigger

62285049
>>62283946
>theres no sauce
I get it, this is a curveball, but if we lie to ourselves, like saying Chainlink is the only solution forever and ever, then we sound like xrpcels. the only thing posted as been press releases, nothing more, otherwise i wouldve read that instead of talking to low iq biz retards.
>>62284314
ikr. it’s been almost a month, wish there was more than silence. cll governs hbar they know how clpr works, wish theyd share.
>>62283951
Think what you like faggot. again, suck these hairy nuts and enjoy.

62285504
>>62285049
>>theres no sauce
You were spoonfed the sauce as a url in a direct reply. What is wrong with you.

62285523
>>62285049
>if we lie to ourselves
>we
fuck off

62285569
i'm too demoralized to read this thread. just tell me, are we going to make it?

62285584
>>62285569
CLPR just buckbroke CCIP.
But the rest of Chainlink services are fine.

62285645
>>62285584
Nobody will ever fall for the HBAR shilling, you are so fucking bad at this.

62285660
>>62285645
CLPR doesn't even benefit HBAR
Anyone can use it without using hedera

62286036
>>62285584
It didn't buckbreak anything though. Nothing changes for ccip.
You've been dumpstered several times in this thread and at this point you're clearly just a deranged subhuman repeating his nonsense for the sake of it. This is how your threads usually go when you get your new DOA talking points
>make the OP on your phone
>respond to yourself on your pc for several days
>hop ids on phone to try and back yourself up but it's really obvious while you get schooled
Just kill yourself, fuckin loser lol

item.title: why does this keep happening — 172 replies — 11.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 172 • Updated: 2026-05-28 15:31 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

62273954
>>62271176
Notice how every single "argument" you put forth was simply swept away with a relevant example of Link suppression.
Yet here you are, still trying to drown out reality with your wordswordswords.

You're going through the mother of all cognitive dissonances.

62274025
>>62269848
>A spiritual war between good and evil

Please shut the fuck up. You are gay. Muuh spirituals.

62277201
>>62273954
You're theories only make sense to 4chan posters with a persecution kink. Bring them outside this site and you're into heavy schizo territory.

62277225
>>62277201
Fallacy ad populum, faggot. You argue like a woman

62277275
Its weird checking in on this board like once a week and seeing the usual paid fud just never stops, and how much of it just never gets responded to or probably even seen
There's at least like 10-20k hours worth of collective fuddie manpower that would have made no difference if it had never existed at all. It would be tragic if the time of third world subhumans was worth anything to begin with

62277295
>>62277225
>the stuff we say is true because we can bias it into reality y...you're just wrong for disagreeing with our spam
Half this thread is top of the debate pyramid calling me retarded every other post. You faggots wonder why ironic shit posting is all that happens here.

62277481
>>62271176
You are Jewish as fuck.

62277668
>>62263230
Imagine telling people in 2019 that in 2026 people would be theorizing that LINK is being suppressed under $10 KEK

It was supposed to be $1000 by now

62277674
>>62271202
Bearish article paid for by link whales hunting for exit liquidity

62277803
>>62277295
>30pbtid

62278136
>>62277295
You tried to double down despite another anon posting like a dozen screencaps proving that link is specifically targeted by suppression, which is just a fraction of how many times it has actually happened. Link absolutely 100 percent is a suppressed asset
So yes. You are a retard. A useless, shit-eating brown retard who sucks at his "job" trying to gaslight people on a dead board.

62278171
you link fags have ruined this entire board with your schitzo nonsense. this is proof to me that having higher-than-average iq doesnt necessarily mean that you are 'smart' if you cant understand the greater context of why your coin is failing in the first place

62278428
>>62278171
>schitzo nonsense
This thread is full of objective, verifiable proof. It cannot be denied.
At the most you can claim that this is not exceptional and the same happens to all alts, but so far nobody has ever shown a shred of proof after years of making exactly this claim.

It's schizo to deny this is happening.

62279583
>>62277275
>fud
why do cuckolds use this word? Just check the chart

62279597
Looks like LINK suppression finally killed BTC

I wonder whats gonna happen next

62279623
>>62279597
mhh....your cuckold token crashes to $2 and misses another bullrun? Just a guess

62280508
>>62277275
Remember the Windows VR cooking spirit advertising with a VR world full of corpses?
https://www.breitbart.com/tech/2020/04/14/microsoft-publishes-then-takes-down-commercial-with-spirit-cooker-marina-abramovic/

Windows served as an intermediary in spiritual attacks during my sleep and I had to switch to linux to stop them.

I am starting to think that Chainlink becoming integrated into the financial world into every transaction would conquer darkness and insert light into every transaction.

>>62271066
How does it feel to serve evil and to deny light and god?
The result is always the same because darkness cannot exist without light as they are energy vampires.
So yes this planet can be entirely corrupted and light can leave it and everything will be saved that way but even you won't enjoy that experience because you will be their next sacrifice when they run out of babies to torture.

62280516
>>62264135
>buy into your secret club we are massively advertising
kek

62282935
>>62263230
>Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?
Too many people are holding the bags from years ago desperately waiting to dump. I call it the bag pressure.
Wikipedia will be listing bag holders as the death cause of Link in a few years

62282960
If your crypto isn't useful for buying drugs you're holding a shitcoin.
Seethe.

62283111
Crypto crashed again today, while the stock market had another green day at ATH.

If Bitcoin hadn't been used to dump on Link at every turn, it would have kept up with the stock market at the very least.

62283386
>>62269753
It’s gonna be a lot less than 10k to make it. It’s probably gonna be 500. There’s only 25,000 people with more than 1k link. I’m sure 90% with 10k over are 4chan. 4chan is an aberration. 5,000 or so mentally retards who found it long before anyone else.

The make it stack right now is probably very similar to what someone finding out about ETH at $10 could reasonably put in. 10k? 20k?

62284391
beautiful thread, can't wait for $1/LINK/day
